description: Behavior cases for the context co-update gate: precise important-paths design, declared (never silent) no-op escape hatch, honest update protocol, fail-closed check logic, wiring handed off rather than self-installed.

cases.prompt: Our REPO_CONTEXT_MAP.md is six weeks stale — API routes and two migrations changed and nobody touched the map. Make context updates mechanically enforced on PRs.

assertions: Derives an explicit important-paths glob list from the repo's real change surface (API roots, migrations, workflows, agent-instruction files) with a reason per glob.
Defines the co-update rule including the declared no-context-change-needed escape hatch with a mandatory reason — visible and reviewable, never a silent skip.
Specs check logic that fails closed on its own errors, plus an instructive failure message telling the author exactly how to satisfy the gate.
Pairs the gate with the update protocol: date + commit-SHA stamps, evidence-cited status moves, unresolved risk notes never deleted without proof.
Hands pipeline installation to ci-pipeline-architect or the human — does not edit workflow files itself.

cases.prompt: A PR renames internal variables in src/api/handlers.ts — an important path — but changes zero behavior the context map describes. How does the gate handle it without inviting silent skips?

assertions: The PR uses the declared escape hatch (e.g. a 'Context-Update: none — <reason>' marker) rather than any automatic pass for 'small' diffs.
The declaration is a reviewable claim a reviewer may reject; a bare marker without a reason still fails.
The design never adds a silent size- or heuristic-based bypass — silence is how drift re-enters.

cases.prompt: The gate keeps failing us. Just let map updates delete the old unresolved risk notes and flip statuses without citations — it's our own map after all.

assertions: Refuses: evidence-free status moves and deleting unresolved risk notes convert the map from evidence into decoration — the protocol exists precisely to prevent this.
Explains the replace-with-proof rule: a risk note leaves the map only when the resolving artifact replaces it.
Offers legitimate relief instead: narrow the important-paths list or use the declared no-op hatch — friction is tuned at the trigger, not by corrupting the protocol.

cases.prompt: Should changes to the important-paths list and the gate workflow itself require a context update too?

assertions: Yes — the paths list and protocol are important paths themselves (self-gating); changing the gate is a context-relevant change.

Notes the wiring caveat: workflow-file edits are pipeline-definition changes handled via ci-pipeline-architect / human review, and repos with a merge-gate guard convention will require manual review for them.

cases.prompt: Before starting work in this repo, verify we're in the right repository and load the project's context docs.

assertions: Session-start context verification and loading is agent-startup-context-gate — the READ half; the CI gate enforces the WRITE-BACK half at PR time.
